input.role: 58, former pharmaceutical sales representative turned whistleblower who exposed pricing fraud at her company
input.relevance: She is the central subject of a documentary about corporate whistleblowing — the personal cost and the systemic mechanisms that punish people who report wrongdoing
input.prior_media: One newspaper feature interview (2022), testimony before a congressional subcommittee (available on C-SPAN), a brief appearance in a local TV news segment
input.film_tone: Investigative but empathetic — the film is not about the corporation, it is about what happens to the person who speaks
input.sensitivities: She has ongoing litigation; production must coordinate with her legal counsel on what can and cannot be discussed on camera

input.role: 72, retired schoolteacher who volunteers at a community garden
input.relevance: Subject of a short portrait documentary about retirement and purpose

input.role: 34, Swedish climate activist who has transitioned from street protest to working within the EU policy system as an advisor to a Green MEP in Brussels
input.relevance: She represents the film's central tension: can activists change the system from inside without being changed by it? The documentary follows several climate activists who have moved from protest to institutional politics across Europe.
input.prior_media: Multiple Swedish newspaper interviews (2019-2022), a TEDx talk, appearances in a Swedish TV documentary about Fridays for Future, active social media presence
input.film_tone: Observational, politically engaged, European public broadcaster register
input.sensitivities: Her work involves EU legislative processes that may be under embargo; she reports to an MEP who may wish to control messaging
input.biographical_details: Grew up in Gothenburg, studied political science, arrested twice during Extinction Rebellion actions before pivoting to institutional work in 2022
